title: lathyrus lanszwertii var. leucanthus
intro: Lathyrus lanszwertii var. leucanthus, also known as the white-flowering sweet pea, is a perennial legume native to the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's seeds contain Lathyrogens, which provide health benefits like reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or powder, with some herbal preparations also using the seeds to make a poultice for treating skin conditions.
